nodes.title: Limited research capacity with systematic methodology development
nodes.text: Imperfect but structured research capabilities focused on continuous methodological improvement

connections.evidence: Historical development of evaluation methodologies in other fields shows iterative improvement possible, though animal welfare evaluation faces unique challenges
connections.assumptions: Research staff can develop domain expertise faster than biases accumulate. Systematic approaches provide value over intuition despite limitations.

nodes.title: Access to animal welfare researchers and practitioners
nodes.text: Relationships with field experts for validation and feedback, with awareness of potential bias

connections.evidence: Expert networks in other evaluation fields provide value through diverse perspectives, though animal welfare field is small and potentially insular
connections.assumptions: Experts provide honest feedback rather than confirmation of existing views. Network diversity sufficient to challenge assumptions.

nodes.title: Systems for tracking prediction accuracy and updating methodology
nodes.text: Mechanisms to measure recommendation performance and improve evaluation methods

connections.evidence: Limited precedent for systematic prediction tracking in nonprofit evaluation, but essential for credibility
connections.assumptions: Organization willing to expose and learn from failures. Sufficient resources available for performance tracking infrastructure.

nodes.title: Explicitly uncertain evaluation frameworks with clear limitations
nodes.text: Structured but imperfect methodologies for organizational assessment, with honest uncertainty communication

connections.evidence: Structured evaluation better than no evaluation in limited studies from other fields, though animal welfare evaluation faces unique measurement challenges
connections.assumptions: Frameworks capture relevant organizational characteristics. Structure improves decision-making despite imperfection.

nodes.title: Systematic tracking of recommendation accuracy and outcomes
nodes.text: Data on how well ACE recommendations predict organizational performance over time

connections.evidence: Feedback loops essential for learning in complex systems, though implementation challenging
connections.assumptions: Organization commits to transparency about failures. Sufficient data available for meaningful pattern detection.

nodes.title: Conditional charity recommendations with explicit uncertainty bounds
nodes.text: Organization assessments presented as uncertain bets rather than confident predictions

connections.evidence: Mixed evidence on whether more information improves donor decisions vs. causing analysis paralysis
connections.assumptions: Donors can process uncertainty information effectively. Explicit limitations increase rather than decrease trust.

nodes.title: Donors make slightly better funding decisions than alternatives
nodes.text: Marginal improvement in resource allocation compared to intuition-based or random giving

connections.evidence: No evidence that systematic nonprofit evaluation improves outcomes compared to alternatives in animal welfare context
connections.assumptions: Better-evaluated organizations actually perform better. Donor behavior changes persist over time. No negative effects from evaluation burden on organizations.

nodes.title: Evaluation methods improve through systematic learning
nodes.text: ACE evaluation accuracy increases over time through performance tracking and methodology updates

connections.evidence: Other evaluation fields show methodological progress possible, though animal welfare presents unique challenges
connections.assumptions: Sufficient feedback available for learning. Organization culture supports updating beliefs based on evidence.

nodes.title: Marginally improved resource allocation within animal advocacy
nodes.text: Slightly more effective distribution of limited resources, with large uncertainty about impact

connections.evidence: No validated connection between improved resource allocation and animal welfare outcomes in current literature
connections.assumptions: Current animal advocacy approaches capable of meaningful impact with better allocation. Scale of movement sufficient to affect animal welfare outcomes.

nodes.title: Possible marginal reduction in animal suffering with high uncertainty
nodes.text: Uncertain and potentially small impact on animal welfare through improved movement effectiveness
